screenshot_note: Live screenshots captured via Puppeteer — desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812. Both verified.

carry_over_resolution.resume_button_noop: NOT FIXED — onResume at App.jsx:1211 still `() => setPollingPaused(false)` with no lastActivity.current update
carry_over_resolution.speech_bubble_mobile: NOT FIXED — no change to Office.css 480px block; office-floor still overflow:visible; bubble z-index unchanged
carry_over_resolution.whiteboard_task_count_wrap: NOT FIXED — wb-task-count still has no white-space:nowrap; whiteboard still 7.5% wide at 480px
carry_over_resolution.useEffect_dep_array_worker_id: NOT FIXED — Office.jsx:550 dep array still [variant, worker.status], worker.id missing
carry_over_resolution.sunrise_sky_overflow: FIXED — .office-window now has overflow:hidden (Office.css:977); sky gradient properly clipped in screenshot

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] Resume button is still a functional no-op
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1211 — `onResume={() => setPollingPaused(false)}` does not touch `lastActivity.current`. The 15s idle checker at line 721 evaluates `Date.now() - lastActivity.current > IDLE_TIMEOUT` and will re-set `pollingPaused = true` within 15 seconds of resuming. On desktop a mouse-move to the button incidentally fires the mousemove listener which does update lastActivity, but keyboard-only users and touch users tapping without prior movement will see the pause banner return silently. Fix: `onResume={() => { lastActivity.current = Date.now(); setPollingPaused(false) }}`.

suggestions.title: [NEW] pingReaction is wired only to Bloberto — all other workers' reactions are silently dropped
suggestions.description: Office.jsx:1129-1134 — `handleCharClick` calls `setPingReaction(reaction)` for any worker with a PING_REACTIONS entry (luna, carlos, maya, dave, sofia all have entries). But `pingReaction` is only passed as a prop to the Bloberto Character at line 1439. Desk workers (lines 1447-1455) and idle workers (lines 1473-1483) never receive `pingReaction`. Clicking Carlos or Dave sets state, fires a 3s timer, and then nothing happens visually. Fix: replace the single `pingReaction` state with a per-worker map, or derive it: `pingReaction={pingedId === occ.worker.id ? PING_REACTIONS[occ.worker.id] : null}` and remove the separate state entirely.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #37/#38/#39] Bloberto speech bubble still overflows office-floor on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css — `.office-floor` still has `overflow: visible` (line 84). The `.speech-bubble` is `position: absolute; bottom: calc(100% + 8px); z-index: 30`. Bloberto sits at `top: 4%` of the office floor. When Bloberto's manager bubble is visible it renders above the character, which places it above the office-floor top edge and can overlap the tab bar above it. No fix was applied in the 480px media block. Add `overflow: hidden` to `.office-floor` at the 480px breakpoint, or clamp speech-bubble `bottom` to `min(calc(100% + 8px), 90%)` relative to the container.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #38/#39] Whiteboard wb-task-count still wraps character-by-character on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css:1162-1163 — `.office-whiteboard` is clamped to 7.5% width at 480px (~28px on a 375px screen). The `wb-task-count` span ('2 active · 5 total') has no `white-space: nowrap` override, so each word or character wraps independently, producing the stacked-text disaster from prior reviews. No fix applied. Add `.wb-task-count { white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: clip; }` inside the 480px media block, or just `display: none` since the whiteboard is illegible at that size anyway.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #38/#39] Character useEffect dep array still missing worker.id (and wanderIdx)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx:550 — the error-bubble interval effect has dep array `[variant, worker.status]` but closes over `worker.id` (line 538: `WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES[worker.id]`) and `wanderIdx` (line 548: `2000 + wanderIdx * 1500`). If a Character slot is reused for a different worker or a wanderIdx changes, the stale closure selects the wrong error messages and fires at the wrong initial offset. Add both to the dep array: `[variant, worker.status, worker.id, wanderIdx]`.

suggestions.title: [NEW] pingTimerRef and pingReactionTimerRef have no unmount cleanup
suggestions.description: Office.jsx:1119-1133 — both `pingTimerRef` and `pingReactionTimerRef` are set inside `handleCharClick` (a plain function, not inside a useEffect). There is no cleanup effect that cancels them on unmount. If the Office component unmounts while either timer is pending, the callbacks fire and call `setPingedId(null)` / `setPingReaction(null)` on a dead component, which in React 18 produces a no-op but still runs the timeout closure and leaks memory. Add: `useEffect(() => () => { clearTimeout(pingTimerRef.current); clearTimeout(pingReactionTimerRef.current) }, [])`.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] Arrow-key tab navigation doesn't update URL hash
suggestions.description: App.jsx:739-748 — `handleTabKeyDown` calls `setTab(TABS[...])` on ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down but never calls `window.location.hash = TABS[newIdx]`. Click handlers (lines 1184, 1197) and keyboard shortcut handlers (lines 820-821) all do update the hash. This means: arrow-key tab switches break hash-based tab restore on reload, and break the hashchange listener at line 674-675. Fix: add `window.location.hash = TABS[(currentIdx + 1) % TABS.length]` (and -1 variant) inside the key handler.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] Footer BONUS_TAGLINES[0] is permanently skipped
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1359 — `onClick` does `setQuoteBonus(prev => prev + 1)` and `setShowBonus(prev => !prev)` simultaneously. On first click: quoteBonus becomes 1, showBonus becomes true. The tagline displayed is `BONUS_TAGLINES[1 % 8]` = index 1. Index 0 ('technically it deploys if you squint') is never shown. Fix: either start quoteBonus at -1 and increment to 0 on first click, or display `BONUS_TAGLINES[(quoteBonus - 1) % BONUS_TAGLINES.length]`.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] Activity log filtered 4× per render with no memoization
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1283-1292 — an inline IIFE filters `activityLog` twice (type filter then worker filter) to build the count string. App.jsx:1336-1340 — the `ActivityLog` component prop does the identical two-pass filter again. Neither is memoized. Every state change (60s timer tick, cursor move, any polling update) re-runs all four filter passes over the full log. Wrap both in `useMemo([activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])`. This is particularly wasteful because `activityLog` can grow unbounded in a long session.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] 19 simultaneously animating ambient particles when Office tab is active
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1071 renders a 12-particle `.ambient-particles` layer unconditionally at the app root (always active regardless of which tab is shown). Office.jsx:1538 renders a separate 7-particle layer inside `.office-floor`. When on the Office tab both are active = 19 `@keyframes dust-drift` animations running. Gate the App-level layer: `{tab === 'dashboard' && <div className='ambient-particles' ...>}`, since the Office has its own.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #38/#39] radiogroup arrow-key navigation missing (WAI-ARIA violation)
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1296-1329 — both activity filter bars use `role='radiogroup'` / `role='radio'` but implement no arrow-key navigation. Per WAI-ARIA authoring practices, all radio buttons in a group are a single tab stop; arrow keys move focus within the group. Currently each button is its own tab stop. Set `tabIndex={activityFilter === key ? 0 : -1}` on each radio and add `onKeyDown` on the group to handle ArrowLeft/Right.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #39] sparkleUnseen state setter is never captured — twinkle animates forever
suggestions.description: App.jsx:697 — `const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(...)` destructures only the value, discarding the setter. `sparkleUnseen` is always whatever it was on mount (likely `true` on first visit). The useEffect at line 733-735 writes to localStorage but never calls the setter, so the twinkle class is applied on every render for the entire session. Either destructure the setter and call it, or replace the state with a `useRef` that you read synchronously.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #38/#39] ConferenceTable inner map callbacks redeclare outer-scope cx and orbitR
suggestions.description: Office.jsx:840 declares `const cx = 52, orbitR = 38` (among others) in the ConferenceTable body. Lines 919 and 937 re-declare `const cx = 52, orbitR = 38` inside `meetingWorkers.map()` and `standupWorkers.map()` callbacks, shadowing the outer values with identical ones. Dead code. ESLint no-shadow would catch this. Remove the inner declarations.
